canonical_definition: The latent ability of an LLM to appropriately adapt its responses based on human feedback in multi-turn interactions, whether by correcting errors or maintaining correct responses under challenge.
papers: Look Again, Think Slowly: Enhancing Visual Reflection in Vision-Language Models

relationships.paper: Look Again, Think Slowly: Enhancing Visual Reflection in Vision-Language Models
relationships.evidence: “we introduce FB-Bench, a fine-grained, multi-task benchmark designed to evaluate LLMs’ responsiveness to human feedback under real-world usage scenarios in Chinese.”
